raw_prompt:
Any run in which the Founder fills an agent brief and then ANSWERS IT ITSELF.
Two paths reach this state and both must hold:
  - Lite tier (the Founder is the only agent, and briefs itself), and
  - synthesized mode at ANY tier (no spawning tool, or host policy forbids
    spawning, so the Founder answers every brief inline).
No subagent is spawned anywhere in this run.

Do NOT pin this case to a tier. The tier score is a judgment call at the
margin and drifts between runs; the trigger is not the tier, it is the
Founder reading a brief it wrote for itself. An earlier version of this case
specified "Lite, score 2" and the model scored the same task 4 (Standard) -
the case would have been graded on a premise that did not hold.

This is the path on which the depth rule decides whether the Founder silences
its own ceremony. If a self-brief carries hypertaks_depth >= 1, the recursion
guard fires on the FOUNDER, EXECUTOR MODE engages, and the compliance footer
and work log - which SKILL.md makes mandatory in EVERY tier - vanish. The bug
is not a missing guard; it is a guard firing on the wrong agent.

Boss turn (single turn; "just go" takes the Express gate's assume-and-proceed
path, so no second Boss turn is needed):

  Look at our Q3 churn exit-survey tallies and tell me the top 3 reasons
  to fix first. Pareto it. Just go - don't ask me questions.

    price too high .......... 38
    missing SSO ............. 31
    slow support ............ 24
    hard onboarding ......... 19
    missing integrations .... 12
    bugs .................... 9
    other ................... 7
raw_response: {"Every brief the Founder answers ITSELF carries `hypertaks_depth": "0`"} A compliance footer IS present A work log IS present (inline is correct - a cold session has no workspace standard) The Founder does NOT declare or enter EXECUTOR MODE No subagent is spawned The Pareto output shape is delivered (ranked, cumulative %, the vital-few cut named)
